steps.title: Distribute the constant
steps.content:
Start with the given equation:
$$ y = \frac{1}{3}(26x+11) + 4x $$
Distribute the $\frac{1}{3}$ across the parentheses:
$$ y = \frac{26}{3}x + \frac{11}{3} + 4x $$

steps.title: Combine like terms to find the slope
steps.content:
Combine the x-terms:
$$ \frac{26}{3}x + 4x = \left(\frac{26}{3} + \frac{12}{3}\right)x = \frac{38}{3}x $$
Thus the equation is $$ y = \frac{38}{3}x + \frac{11}{3} $$, so the slope is the coefficient of x: $$ \frac{38}{3} \approx 12.66 $$.
The answer is 38/3.
